title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
One-Time Investment for a Child
As a parent, it is your duty to plan for your child’s future to stay prepared for major milestones, such as school fees, higher education, or health. With rising education and lifestyle costs, parents are looking for smart ways to secure long-term stability early on. A well-chosen one-time investment for child is one of the ways to create a financial cushion that grows quietly in the background. Let us know about the various available plans and how to choose them based on your financial goals, risk tolerance, and time horizon.
What Is a One-Time Investment Plan for a Child?
As the name suggests, a one-time investment plan is one where you invest a lump sum amount in a particular plan, rather than making monthly premiums. Some good options for a one-time investment plan for a newborn baby are:
Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
SSY is a dedicated savings scheme for girls, providing parents a safe, government-backed way to plan for their future. It offers one of the highest small-savings interest rates along with tax benefits. You can invest once, every year and grow a stable corpus for your child’s needs. The account stays active for 21 years, which is calculated from the account opening date or until the marriage of the girl, whichever happens first, allowing the money to grow steadily over time.
PPF for Minors
A PPF account for a minor works as a stable, long-term savings tool with a 15-year lock-in period and tax advantages. A yearly contribution is needed to keep the account active. You can open the account on behalf of your child, and while partial withdrawals are allowed in specific cases, the funds must always support the child’s needs.
Mutual Funds for Children
These funds operate similarly to regular mutual funds but are specifically designed to support future goals, such as education or skill development. Investing a lump sum in equity or hybrid investments, depending on risk appetite, can allow long-term growth.
Fixed Deposits
FDs allow parents to invest a lump sum at a predetermined interest rate, making them a reliable choice for conservative investors. They typically offer better returns than savings accounts and ensure capital safety.
Why Parents Choose One-Time Investments for Child Planning?
Here’s why parents choose child insurance plans for their children’s future:
Building a Financial Base for Education
Rising education costs make early planning essential. One-time investments help parents accumulate a solid fund over time, and some plans even allow partial withdrawals to meet expenses as they arise.
Protection Against Life Uncertainties
Several child insurance plans include a waiver of premium feature that keeps the policy going even after the parent’s death. This ensures that the child continues to receive all promised benefits, giving families peace of mind.
Funding Key Life Milestones
Whether it’s higher studies, extracurricular growth, or future life events, these investments create a ready pool of money when the child needs it most. Tax benefits on eligible plans also make them more attractive for long-term planning.
Types of One-Time Investment Options Available for Children
There are various long-term investment plans for children to choose from, as per your financial goals and risk tolerance.
Insurance-Linked One-Time Investment Plans
These plans offer dual benefits of a life cover and building a corpus for the future. There are two types of savings-oriented life insurance plans that you can consider -
Endowment Plans:
If you prefer a low-risk option that offers insurance coverage and stable savings, endowment plans can prove suitable. Some plans also add bonuses over the years, helping the amount grow safely.
Money Back Plans:
If you want insurance protection but also prefer getting payouts at regular intervals, you may choose this plan. Instead of waiting until the end of the term, the policy pays small portions of the sum assured during the policy tenure, giving you steady liquidity for school fees, classes, or other needs. Even after these payouts, the full sum assured is still paid to the nominee in case of the insured’s death.
Market-Linked Long-Term Investment Options
These plans offer market-linked returns, and some of the common options are as follows -
ULIPs (Unit Linked Insurance Plans):
ULIPs mix protection and investment in one plan. A portion of your premium is used for life cover, and the rest is invested in different types of funds based on your risk appetite.You can switch between funds if needed, withdraw partially after the lock-in, and let your money grow with the market. A ULIP calculator helps estimate returns, helping you plan a suitable corpus for your child.
Mutual Funds for Children:
Child-focused mutual fund schemes encourage long-term wealth creation through disciplined investing. They usually come with a lock-in of five years or until the child becomes an adult.This longer horizon allows fund managers to grow your money through compounding over the years, making it a solid choice for education or milestone planning.
Direct Equity (Shares):
Buying shares directly can help you build wealth faster, but it demands knowledge and tolerance for high risk. There’s no lock-in, so you can exit anytime, and returns come through capital gains or dividends. It is suitable if you want market-linked growth and have the time to research stocks.
Child-Focused Plans with Protection
These include child insurance plans offered by life insurers. Child plans can be offered as traditional savings plans (endowment or money back) or market-linked plans (ULIPs). Child plans usually offer the premium waiver feature, which waives the premiums if the parent is no longer around. The plan runs unaffected and pays a maturity benefit to the child so that their future financial needs can be met.
Benefits of a One-Time Investment for a Child’s Future
Investing a lump sum in a child future plans not only secures their future but also allows you to fulfil their dreams. Here are some benefits of a one-time investment:
Long-Term Wealth Creation
A one-time investment gives your money a head start because the entire amount begins compounding from the very first day. You don’t have to worry about monthly payments or reminders, while your lump sum quietly grows in the background. Over the years, this early boost has helped build a strong corpus for major goals, such as your child’s education or other significant milestones.
Tax Benefits Under the Income Tax Act
Many child-focused investment plans, especially insurance policies, offer attractive tax advantages. You can claim deductions on your investment under the provisions of the Income Tax Act, 1961. Plus, the returns, whether maturity payouts or death benefits, can also enjoy tax exemption up to the permitted limits, depending on the type of plan selected, helping you keep more of what you earn for your child’s future.
One-Time Investment Planning for a Newborn Baby
Starting early helps your money grow steadily and gives you more flexibility as your child’s needs evolve.
Starting Early to Maximise Compounding
When you begin investing right from your baby’s early years, your money gets more time to compound. This allows you to choose moderately risky options without stress. Early investing also gives you room to adjust your strategy if something doesn’t go as expected.
Aligning Investments with a Child’s Age
It helps to break your child’s financial needs into short-term and long-term goals. Expenses like school fees or hobby classes fall under short-term needs and can be handled through short-term investment options. Bigger goals, such as higher education, overseas studies, or marriage, require long-term planning. Starting early lets you balance both smartly.
How to Choose the Right One-Time Investment Plan for Your Child?
Below are some factors that you should consider while planning for your child’s future:
Risk Appetite and Investment Horizon
Start by matching the plan with your child’s timeline. If your child is 6 and you need funds at 12, choose an option that matures around that year. For goals such as higher education or overseas studies, you choose long-term investment plans.
Invest based on your risk comfort: safer choices offering stable returns are for low-risk parents, while market-linked options may work for those who can handle market fluctuations.
Flexibility, Withdrawals and Riders
Long-term plans often come with lock-in periods, so it’s wise to check how easily you can access funds when needed. Choosing investment options that allow partial withdrawals, especially for education or medical needs, can be a lifesaver. Flexibility matters just as much as returns when planning for your child’s future.
If you want extra protection, consider adding riders for accidental cover, critical illness, or income support to your child insurance plans. They strengthen your child’s financial safety net without changing the core plan.
Key Takeaway
One-time investment for child planning helps you secure your child’s future early with a single lump-sum contribution.
You can choose from several options based on your goals and risk levels.
One-time investment helps to build education funds, handle uncertainties, and support big milestones without financial stress.
You can pick between safer plans like endowment or money-back policies and market-linked choices like ULIPs, mutual funds, and shares. These investments create long-term wealth, offer tax benefits, and support early planning, especially when aligned with your child’s age and goals.
Choosing the right plan means aligning with risk levels, investment horizon, flexibility, withdrawal rules, and riders that protect your child's future.
Conclusion
Planning a one-time investment for a child gives you a simple, focused way to secure your child’s future without constant payments or stress. When you choose the right mix of safety, growth, and flexibility, your money quietly builds over the years and supports every milestone, from school needs to higher education to marriage. Starting early and picking suitable plans ensures your child has a strong financial cushion when it matters most.
Frequently Asked Questions
What is the ideal age to start a one-time investment for a child?
While you can begin investing at any age, starting right from your child’s birth gives you leverage. Starting early gives your money more years to grow through compounding, allowing you to pick long-term options like SSY, PPF, or mutual funds with ease.
Are one-time investment plans for children tax-efficient?
Yes, many one-time investment plans for children (especially insurance policies) are tax-friendly. You can claim deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakh a year under Section 80C, and in most cases, the maturity payout also enjoys tax exemption.
Can one-time investments be partially withdrawn for emergencies?
Yes, some long-term investments allow partial withdrawals in the event of an emergency, depending on the plan you choose. Options like ULIPs, child mutual fund schemes, and even PPF (after specific years) allow you access to some funds when needed.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
One-Time Investment for a Child
As a parent, it is your duty to plan for your child’s future to stay prepared for major milestones, such as school fees, higher education, or health. With rising education and lifestyle costs, parents are looking for smart ways to secure long-term stability early on. A well-chosen one-time investment for child is one of the ways to create a financial cushion that grows quietly in the background. Let us know about the various available plans and how to choose them based on your financial goals, risk tolerance, and time horizon.
What Is a One-Time Investment Plan for a Child?
As the name suggests, a one-time investment plan is one where you invest a lump sum amount in a particular plan, rather than making monthly premiums. Some options for a one-time investment plan for a newborn baby are:
1. Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
SSY is a dedicated savings scheme for girls under the age of 10 years , providing parents a safe, government-backed way to plan for their future.1 It offers attractive interest rates along with tax benefits. You can invest once, every year and grow a stable corpus for your child’s needs. The account requires deposits for 15 years. The tenure is calculated from the account opening date or until the marriage of the girl, whichever happens first, allowing the money to grow steadily over time.3
2. PPF for Minors2
A PPF account for a minor works as a stable, long-term savings tool with a 15-year lock-in period and tax advantages. You can open the account on behalf of your child, and while partial withdrawals are allowed in specific cases, the funds must always support the child’s needs. 3. Mutual Funds for Children
These funds operate similarly to regular mutual funds but are specifically designed to support future goals, such as education or skill development. 4. Fixed Deposits
FDs allow parents to invest a lump sum at a predetermined interest rate, making them a reliable choice for conservative investors.
Why Parents Choose One-Time Investments for Child Planning?
Here’s why parents choose child insurance plans for their children’s future:
1. Building a Financial Base for Education
Rising education costs make early planning essential. One-time investments may help parents accumulate a sizeable corpus over time, and some plans even allow partial withdrawals to meet expenses as they arise.
2. Protection Against Life Uncertainties
Several child insurance plans include a waiver of premium feature that keeps the policy going even after the parent’s death. This ensures that the child continues to receive all promised benefits, giving families peace of mind. 3. Funding Key Life Milestones
Whether it’s higher studies, extracurricular growth, or future life events, these investments create a ready pool of money when the child needs it most. Tax benefits on eligible plans also make them more attractive for long-term planning.
Types of One-Time Investment Options Available for Children
There are various long-term investment plans for children to choose from, as per your financial goals and risk tolerance.
1. Life Insurance Plans
These plans offer dual benefits of a life cover and building a corpus for the future. Below are the types of life insurance plans that you can consider . These plans can be considered as one time investment plans if the premium is paid in a lump sum at the beginning of the policy-
o Endowment Plans:
If you prefer a low-risk option that offers life insurance coverage and savings, endowment plans can prove suitable. Some plans also offer bonuses (if declared) over the years, helping the amount grow safely. o Money Back Plans:
If you want life insurance coverage but also prefer getting payouts at regular intervals, you may choose this plan. The policy pays small portions of the sum assured during the policy tenure called as the survival benefit, giving you steady liquidity for school fees, classes, or other financial needs. Even after these payouts, the full sum assured is paid to the nominee in case of the life assured’s death.
o ULIPs (Unit Linked Insurance Plans):
ULIPs are life insurance plans which offer the dual benefit of life insurance cover along with the potential of wealth creation through market linked returns in one plan. A portion of your premium is used for life cover, and the rest is invested in different types of market linked funds based on your risk appetite. You can switch between market linked funds if needed, it also allows you to partially withdraw from your fund value post the lock in period in case of exigencies. A ULIP calculator helps estimate returns, helping you plan a suitable corpus for your child. 2. Market-Linked Long-Term Investment Options
These plans offer market-linked returns, and some of the common options are as follows -
o Mutual Funds for Children:
Child-focused mutual fund schemes encourage long-term wealth creation through disciplined investing. They usually come with a lock-in of five years or until the child becomes an adult. This longer horizon allows fund managers to grow your money through compounding over the years, making it a popular choice for education or milestone planning. o Direct Equity (Shares):
Buying shares directly can help you build wealth faster, but it demands knowledge and tolerance for high risk. There’s no lock-in, so you can exit anytime, and returns come through capital gains or dividends. It is suitable if you want market-linked growth and have the time to research stocks. 3. Child-Focused Plans for Financial Protection
These include child based insurance plans offered by life insurers. Child based insurance plans can be offered as traditional savings plans (endowment or money back) or market- linked plans (ULIPs). Child based life insurance plans usually offer the waiver of premium feature, which waives the premiums in circumstances such as the parents no longer being around . The plan runs unaffected and pays a maturity benefit to the child so that their future financial needs can be met.
Benefits of One-Time Investment for a Child’s Future
Investing a lump sum in a child’s future plan not only secures their future but also allows you to fulfil their dreams. Here are some of the benefits of a one-time investment:
1. Long-Term Wealth Creation
A one-time investment gives your money a head start because the entire amount begins compounding from the very first day. You don’t have to worry about monthly payments or reminders, while your lump sum quietly grows in the background. Over the years, this early boost has helped build a sizeable corpus for major goals, such as your child’s education or other significant milestones. 2. Tax Benefits Under the Income Tax Act,1961
Many child-focused investment plans, especially life insurance policies, offer attractive tax advantages. You can claim deductions on your investment under the provisions of the Income Tax Act, 1961. Plus, the returns, whether maturity payouts or death benefits, can also enjoy tax exemption up to the permitted limits, depending on the type of plan selected, helping you keep more of what you earn for your child’s future.
One-Time Investment Planning for a Newborn Baby
Starting early helps your money grow steadily and gives you more flexibility as your child’s financial needs evolve.
1. Starting Early to Maximise Compounding
When you begin investing right from your baby’s early years, your money gets more time to compound. This allows you to choose moderately risky options without stress. Early investing also gives you room to adjust your strategy if something doesn’t go as expected. 2. Aligning Investments with a Child’s Age
It helps to break your child’s financial needs into short-term and long-term goals. Expenses like school fees or hobby classes fall under short-term needs and can be handled through short-term investment options. Bigger goals, such as higher education, overseas studies, or marriage, require long-term planning. Starting early lets you balance both smartly.
How to Choose the Right One-Time Investment Plan for Your Child?
Below are some factors that you should consider while planning for your child’s future:
1. Risk Appetite and Investment Horizon
Start by matching the plan with your child’s timeline. If your child is 6 and you need the corpus at 12, choose an option that matures around that year. For goals such as higher education or overseas studies, you choose long-term investment plans. Invest based on your risk appetite : safer choices offering stable returns are for parents who want to avoid risks, while market-linked options may work for those who can handle market fluctuations. 2. Flexibility, Partial Withdrawals and Riders
Long-term plans often come with lock-in periods, so it’s wise to check how easily you can access the corpus when needed. Choosing investment options that allow partial withdrawals, especially for education or medical needs, can be a lifesaver. Flexibility matters just as much as returns when planning for your child’s future.
If you want added financial protection, consider adding riders for accidental cover, critical illness, or income support to your child based life insurance plans. These riders incur a nominal additional premium and are subject to availability with the base plan that you choose. They strengthen your child’s financial safety net.
Key Takeaway
1. One-time investment for child planning helps you secure your child’s future early with a
single lump-sum contribution. 2. You can choose from several options based on your goals and risk levels. 3. One-time investment helps to build education funds, handle uncertainties, and support big
milestones without financial stress. 4. You can pick between plans like endowment or money-back plans and market-linked
choices like ULIPs, mutual funds, and shares. These investments create long-term wealth,
offer tax benefits, and support early planning, especially when aligned with your child’s age and goals. 5. Choosing the right plan means aligning with risk levels, investment horizon, flexibility,
withdrawal rules, and riders that protect your child's future.
Conclusion
Planning a one-time investment for a child gives you a simple, focused way to secure your child’s future without constant payments or stress. When you choose the right mix of safety, growth, and flexibility, your money quietly builds over the years and supports every milestone, from school needs to higher education to marriage. Starting early and picking suitable plans ensures your child has a strong financial cushion when it matters most.
Frequently Asked Questions
1. What is the ideal age to start a one-time investment for a child?
While you can begin investing at any age, starting right from your child’s birth gives you leverage. Starting early gives your money more years to grow through compounding, allowing you to pick long-term options like SSY, PPF, life insurance plans or mutual funds with ease. 2. Are one-time investment plans for children tax-efficient?
Yes, many one-time investment plans for children (especially life insurance policies) are tax- friendly. You can claim deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakh a year under Section 80C, and in most cases, the maturity payout also enjoys tax exemption. 3. Can one-time investments be partially withdrawn for emergencies?
Yes, some long-term investments allow partial withdrawals in the event of an emergency, depending on the plan you choose. Options like ULIPs, child mutual fund schemes, and even PPF (after specific years) allow you access to some funds when needed. However, there might be a lock-in period during which partial withdrawals would not be allowed , so be sure to review these conditions beforehand.
Disclaimer

changes.word_original: Child-Focused Plans with Protection
changes.pdf_final: Child-Focused Plans for Financial Protection

changes.word_original: These include child insurance plans offered by life insurers.
changes.pdf_final: These include child based insurance plans offered by life insurers.

changes.word_original: Child plans can be offered as traditional savings plans (endowment or money back) or market-linked plans (ULIPs).
changes.pdf_final: Child based insurance plans can be offered as traditional savings plans (endowment or money back) or market- linked plans (ULIPs).

changes.word_original: Child plans usually offer the premium waiver feature, which waives the premiums if the parent is no longer around.
changes.pdf_final: Child based life insurance plans usually offer the waiver of premium feature, which waives the premiums in circumstances such as the parents no longer being around .

changes.word_original: Benefits of a One-Time Investment for a Child’s Future
changes.pdf_final: Benefits of One-Time Investment for a Child’s Future

changes.word_original: Investing a lump sum in a child future plans not only secures their future but also allows you to fulfil their dreams.
changes.pdf_final: Investing a lump sum in a child’s future plan not only secures their future but also allows you to fulfil their dreams.

changes.word_original: Here are some benefits of a one-time investment:
changes.pdf_final: Here are some of the benefits of a one-time investment:

changes.word_original: Over the years, this early boost has helped build a strong corpus for major goals, such as your child’s education or other significant milestones.
changes.pdf_final: Over the years, this early boost has helped build a sizeable corpus for major goals, such as your child’s education or other significant milestones. 2.

changes.word_original: Tax Benefits Under the Income Tax Act
changes.pdf_final: Tax Benefits Under the Income Tax Act,1961

changes.word_original: Many child-focused investment plans, especially insurance policies, offer attractive tax advantages.
changes.pdf_final: Many child-focused investment plans, especially life insurance policies, offer attractive tax advantages.

changes.pdf_final: Starting early helps your money grow steadily and gives you more flexibility as your child’s financial needs evolve.

changes.word_original: Early investing also gives you room to adjust your strategy if something doesn’t go as expected.
changes.pdf_final: Early investing also gives you room to adjust your strategy if something doesn’t go as expected. 2.

changes.pdf_final: If your child is 6 and you need the corpus at 12, choose an option that matures around that year.

changes.word_original: Invest based on your risk comfort: safer choices offering stable returns are for low-risk parents, while market-linked options may work for those who can handle market fluctuations.
changes.pdf_final: Invest based on your risk appetite : safer choices offering stable returns are for parents who want to avoid risks, while market-linked options may work for those who can handle market fluctuations. 2.
